meta_title: For Freelancers: marketing automation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Marketing Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use marketing automation to overcome a lack of portfolio. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for fast, professional results.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build a high-utility marketing automation tool that solves a specific business pain point. By using Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to handle the logic, you create a tangible asset that demonstrates technical competence, speed, and real-world value to prospective clients.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, the dreaded 'zero-client' void looming large. Every client asks for a portfolio you don't have, and every project you want requires a portfolio you can't build without the client. It is the classic freelancer's paradox: you need the work to get the work. But the game has changed—you no longer need to wait for a client to build a showcase piece.

Today, we pivot from 'waiting for work' to 'shipping solutions.' By building a marketing automation tool, you demonstrate your ability to drive revenue—the single most important metric for any business. We will use Antigravity to structure the build and Claude Code to execute the logic, turning your lack of experience into a display of high-octane engineering efficiency.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Workflow
steps.description: Identify a repetitive manual task, like lead scraping or email sequencing, that business owners hate. We will automate this to prove ROI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project architecture for a marketing automation tool that scrapes LinkedIn leads and sends personalized email outreach via Gmail API. Include folder structure for services, controllers, and config.

steps.title: Build the Scraper Engine
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build a robust scraper that gathers potential leads, ensuring it follows ethical best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular scraper service using Playwright that extracts name, company, and email from professional profiles, handling rate limits gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Implement the Playwright scraper logic in src/services/scraper.ts with error handling for 429 status codes'

steps.title: Integrate Email Automation
steps.description: Connect the data to an email provider so the tool doesn't just collect data, but acts on it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a controller that takes the scraped list and triggers personalized email drafts using the Gmail API, including a template engine.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Add the Gmail API integration to src/services/mailer.ts and create a template engine for dynamic placeholders'

steps.title: Implement Performance Logging
steps.description: Add logging to track success rates, which you will later use as 'proof of concept' in your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a logging middleware that tracks the number of emails sent and successful responses in a JSON-based database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Add winston logging and a local JSON database to track outreach performance metrics'

steps.title: Deploy and Showcase
steps.description: Deploy the tool and record a 60-second Loom video demonstrating the automation in action—this is your new portfolio piece.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md file that highlights the business impact of this tool, including installation steps and a 'Why this matters' section.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a professional README.md that positions this tool as a lead generation accelerator for small businesses'

pro_tips: Focus on the 'Business Value' in your README; clients care about revenue, not your clean code architecture.
Use a real-world edge case as a 'feature' to show you can handle production-level errors.
Record a screen-share of the automation actually running—it's 10x more persuasive than a static GitHub link.
Always include a 'Cost Saved' calculation in your project description to quantify your impact.
Keep your repository public but add a 'Configuration' file placeholder to protect sensitive API keys.
content.next_steps: Once your tool is live, reach out to three local businesses offering a free trial of your automation. Use their feedback to iterate, turning this project into a case study with real results.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the technical edge to build high-value tools that command premium rates, regardless of your past experience. Stop waiting for permission and start building the future of your freelance career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
